title: Crash
tagline: Ride the rising multiplier; cash out before it busts.
mechanic: Each tick survives with probability p (volatility). The multiplier rises to RTP ÷ p^ticks; a bust wipes it. Same survival-ladder math as Chicken Road, drawn as a curve. Progression is client-driven because open-rgs has no in-process timers — a real live-crash needs a time oracle.
showcases: tick-based complex round (client-paced, no server timer)
the survival-ladder family, framed as a curve
mathTest: Constant-EV ladder: cashing at any tick has expected value RTP, so the measured RTP is 0.99 regardless of when the simulator's strategy bails.
